title: Pro subscribers churning after first 3 months at 2x rate of non-Pro users
description: Pro subscriber retention drops sharply after month 3, with churn rate 2x higher than non-Pro users in the same period. We need to understand whether this is an onboarding problem, a value perception gap, or a competitive alternative pulling users away.

assumptions.content: Pro subscribers don't discover enough value features in the first 90 days
Cashback and free delivery are not differentiated enough vs competitors

kill_signals: Churn is driven by price sensitivity alone — no UX intervention viable

refinement_history.detail: Added L2a-pro context layer showing current Pro value proposition and competitive landscape
Reframed from 'improve Pro retention' to specific churn window (month 3) based on data context
